title: repo_shortlist
description: Ranked repos worth contributing to, with the single best issue candidate per repo. Consumed by opensource-contributor and the contribute/contribution-fleet commands, which bind a target via .repos[0].repo. Owner (sole writer): repo-finder.

criteria.description: the RESOLVED filter that produced this list — what actually ran, from parse_preferences, not what was intended. A scan that searched Python when the user asked for Go is then visible in the file rather than three scans later.

profile.description: user = ~/.superhuman/preferences.md; default = DEFAULT_PROFILE (no preferences file); override = per-run --lang/--topic/--min-stars

languages.description: empty means any language. Languages OR inside one query, so they never add queries.

topics.description: one search query per entry. `any` is reserved and compiles to a query with no topic: qualifier.

min_stars.description: per-entry star floor; overrides min_stars for this topic only
a floor. There is no ceiling: no ranges, no maximum, anywhere.

queries.description: the literal q= strings sent to search/repositories

coverage.description: How much of the candidate space was actually scored, so a partial scan is visible in the artifact rather than only in the agent's prose. `scored` much smaller than `survivors` means repos were never evaluated and a good lower-ranked repo may be missing — a fact a downstream consumer, or the user, can only act on if it is written here.

candidates.description: unique repos after dedup across all queries

survivors.description: candidates that passed the Step 2 fast filter

scored.description: survivors actually scored in Step 3; must equal survivors unless the deterministic Step 1 cap truncated the set

repo.description: owner/repo slug. Search returns this field as full_name; map it across on write or .repos[0].repo binds null downstream.

language.description: null for repos GitHub cannot classify

topics.description: from the Step 1 search projection; cited by the notes rationale

scores.description: commands/repo-finder.md renders scores.final. A score written to a bare .score key renders as null on every row.
